free_text: Measurements of proton-induced radionuclide
production cross sections to evaluate cosmic-ray
activation of tellurium
NASA-Marshall Space Flight Center,Huntsville,
Alabama
Universidad de Zaragoza,Zaragoza
Irradiations at the energy of 800 MeV
were performed in the 'Blue Room' at the Los Alamos
National Laboratory's Neutron Science Center
Irradiations at the energy of 1.4 GeV
were conducted at CERN ISOLDE facility using the
RABBIT system. Exposures were also carried out in air
with the 23 GeV beam at the IRRADIATION 1 Facility at
the CERN PS East Hall.
At 800 Mev the Te target was fabricated from 99.9999%
pure Te Alpha Aesar metallic powder that was pressed
into a 6.9-mm thick disk that had 93.4% of normal Te
metal density.A stack consisted of 6.4 mm thick
polyethylene in front of the Te target and then an
aluminum backing of 2 mm thickness. Polyethylene and
Al foils served for beam monitoring purposes.
A telluride crystals TeO2 with mass of 0.457 g and
irregular shape was irradiated at the 1.4 GeV beam of
ISOLDE. At the energy of 23 GeV two samples were
used. A crystal of TeO2 of irregular shape with a
mass of 0.428 g and a larger TeO2 crystal of 23x22 mm
sides and 1.6 mm thickness.
Several HPGe detectors were used to measure
induced activity of irradiated samples with
efficiency from 25% relative efficiency n-type
coaxial high purity germanium detector to 80%
relative efficiency high purity germanium detector
used at LBNL's Low Background Facility. Samples
irradiated at CERN were measured using 40% efficiency
detector in CERN and later using 30% to 60% relative
efficiency detectors at the low- radioactivity
laboratory of INFN and the University of
Milano-Bicocca.
Samples were irradiated for several minutes.
For measurement of short lived products the delay
times between irradiation and activity measurements
varied from 1200 sec to 10 hours and counting times
from 1000 sec to 10 days. Long lived products were
measured for period up to several days after delay
varying from 16  days to several years for a period
of several days up to 1 year.
No information given
Compiled by S.H.
